info.name: Referral — Doctor / Specialist (Named, Targeted)
info.description:
### Quick-start: environment setup

1. Import **Referral_Dev_Environment.json** into Postman (Environments -> Import).
2. Select the **Referral Dev** environment from the environment picker.
3. Set `bpp_uri`, `bap_uri`, and `auth_token` for your sandbox. `bpp_uri` points at the
   Care Coordinator for the T1 folder and at the Referred Provider for the T2 folder —
   switch it between the two transactions.
4. `network_id` is currently `example.net/health` (placeholder) — confirm with the registry team.
5. UUID variable defaults are pre-filled; `coordination_id` is the load-bearing link
   (T1 issues it, T2 carries it as `coordinationRef`). Keep it identical across both folders.

## Referral — Doctor / Specialist (Named, Targeted)

**Scenario:** A GP (Dr. Priya Anand) refers patient Ravi Kumar to a named cardiologist
(Dr. Mehta) via a Care Coordinator. Fully targeted: the referral names the provider.

**Two-transaction model**
- **T1 — Referral Coordination:** BAP = Referrer (GP NP), BPP = Care Coordinator (CC).
  `contract.contractAttributes` = **HealthReferral**. coordinationId issued at on_confirm = the Referral ID.
- **T2 — Appointment / Booking:** BAP = the SAME CC (now Seeker NP), BPP = Referred Provider.
  `contract.contractAttributes` = **HealthContract** with `coordinationRef = {{coordination_id}}`.

**Targeting mode:** Named / targeted — `recommendedTargetRef` PRESENT (Dr. Mehta snapshot).
T2 **skips discover** and selects the named provider directly.

**Consent class:** B (treatment-relationship) — precondition for T1 on_confirm.

**healthServiceType:** PHYSICAL_CONSULTATION. Outcome note delivered at T2 on_status.

**Deviation shown:** NO_SHOW — T2 `update` (attendanceStatus NO_SHOW) then T1 `update`
lifecycleState=NO_SHOW + deviation.reasonCode NO_SHOW.

**T1 -> T2 link:** `coordination_id` (T1 on_confirm) is carried into T2 as
`contractAttributes.coordinationRef` and back-linked as T1 `targetBookingRef = {{appointment_id}}`.

item.name: T1 · Referral Coordination  (BAP Referrer → BPP Care Coordinator)
item.description: Named/targeted referral. recommendedTargetRef carries the Dr. Mehta provider+offer snapshot from Discover 1. Consent Class B (treatment-relationship). coordinationId issued at on_confirm = Referral ID.

request.description: Referrer discovers a Care Coordinator able to handle this referral category.
Care Coordinator returns its coordination catalogue entry.
Referrer originates DRAFT coordination contract (no id; CC assigns at on_select).
CC accepts; assigns coordination contract id. (= the Referral ID at on_confirm.)
Referrer confirms binding intent; full participant detail supplied.
CC returns binding quote for the coordination fee.
Referrer confirms. CONSENT (class B) is the precondition for issuance.
★ CC issues the Referral. coordinationId = {{coordination_id}} is the persistent Referral ID, consumed by T2 as coordinationRef.
Referrer polls coordination state.
CC reports lifecycleState BOOKING_CONFIRMED; targetBookingRef = {{appointment_id}}.

item.name: T2 · Appointment / Booking  (BAP Care Coordinator → BPP Referred Provider)
item.description: CC books the NAMED provider directly — **discover is skipped** (select straight to Dr. Mehta). coordinationRef links to T1. Consultation outcome note returned at on_status.

request.description: CC books the NAMED provider directly via select (discover skipped). coordinationRef = {{coordination_id}} links this booking to T1.
Provider accepts; assigns appointment contract id.
CC confirms binding intent; referral note handed over via descriptor.docs[] + inputArtifacts.
Provider returns binding quote and confirmed appointment slot.
CC confirms the appointment.
★ Provider confirms appointment {{appointment_id}}. CC then sets T1 lifecycleState BOOKING_CONFIRMED.
CC polls the appointment / awaits outcome.
★ Provider reports ATTENDED and delivers the consultation outcome note (outcomeNoteRef). CC projects outcome onto T1 (OUTCOME_RECEIVED).

item.name: Deviation — NO_SHOW
item.description: **Deviation: NO_SHOW.** Provider records NO_SHOW on the T2 appointment via `update`; the CC mirrors it onto T1 with lifecycleState=NO_SHOW and deviation.reasonCode=NO_SHOW.

request.description: Patient No-Show on the T2 appointment.
Provider acknowledges the T2 deviation.
CC mirrors the deviation onto T1: lifecycleState=NO_SHOW, deviation.reasonCode=NO_SHOW.
Referrer acknowledges the updated referral lifecycle.

variable.description: Confirm exact value with the network registry team.
Target BPP base URL for the active transaction (CC for T1, Provider for T2).
BAP callback base URL for on_* simulations.
Care Coordinator NP (BPP in T1, BAP in T2).
T1 coordination contract id = Referral ID. Consumed by T2 as coordinationRef.
